source.title: Encoding of conditioned inhibitors of fear in the infralimbic cortex

confidence_rationale: Single-unit recordings show IL neurons encode conditioned inhibitors specifically -- distinct from reward-cue encoding. Three subpopulations: (1) neurons responding exclusively to the fear+safety compound, (2) neurons responding to both fear+safety and reward cues, (3) bidirectional neurons (excited by fear+safety, inhibited by fear cue). Negative correlation between IL excitation and freezing is the functional linkage. This is direct electrophysiological evidence that the IL encodes safety-cue-specific information and that this encoding is dissociable from reward. The dissociation from reward-cue responses supports MECH-303's claim of a parallel-but-distinct store.
failure_signatures: A subpopulation of IL neurons responded to both the fear+safety cue AND the reward cue, suggesting partial overlap between the safety-prediction store and the reward store -- this is a direct qualifier on MECH-303's 'distinct from the things-liked store' claim.

Neural activity was measured in response to the compound fear+safety cue, not to the safety cue alone. Most neurons responded to the compound but not to safety-alone, so the predictive encoding during safety-only contexts (the MECH-303 target) is not directly measured.
Only IL was recorded; no simultaneous recording from ventral hippocampus or prefrontal subregions that would confirm the vHipp-PL pathway that MECH-303 also posits.

mapping.source_claim_statement: IL neurons show excitatory responses specifically to conditioned inhibitors (fear+safety compound cue), with activity negatively correlated with fear expression. A subset also responds to reward cues. Neural encoding is safety-cue-specific and distinct from pure fear or pure reward responses.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-303 requires a prefrontal-hippocampal encoding substrate that stores 'this context predicts suffering will be absent'. The IL single-unit data establish that IL encodes conditioned inhibition as an active representation -- not merely a passive absence of fear-cue responding. The partial overlap with reward-responding neurons is a complication: MECH-303 asserts the safety store is parallel-but-distinct from the reward store, which this data qualifies (some neurons are shared).
mapping.mapping_caveat: The compound-cue paradigm conflates the predictive encoding of safety with the conflict-resolution computation (safety overriding fear). MECH-303 is primarily a claim about the predictive safety store (what encodes future safety), not necessarily about the conflict-resolution node.
mapping.source_context: Male Long Evans rats, single-unit recordings in IL, Pavlovian conditional discrimination task with fear, reward, and safety cues.

confidence_components.notes: High quality electrophysiology from a well-characterised paradigm. Mapping fidelity is good but docked because the compound-cue context differs from the standalone safety-cue prediction scenario MECH-303 targets. The partial overlap with reward neurons is a genuine qualifier on the 'distinct' claim, recorded directly in the data.
